title: [突發] China Build Underwater Surveillance Platform

replies.msg:
China Build Underwater Surveillance Platform for Submarine Anti-Access

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zjhi0oaWfuA



Published on Mar 2, 2017
Breaking News - China Build Underwater Surveillance Platform for Submarine Anti-Access

Last month, China’s media reported that Beijing planned to ban submarines from East and South China Sea. But many were clueless on how China can achieve this in the international water. However, recent development has given some clues. China could build underwater surveillance platform to prevent access any submarine entering the prohibited region.

China did not say that exactly, but anyone with some intelligent can conclude that China will build an underwater platform for military purpose, under the pretext of scientific researches. However, news circulated that China will build its first long-term underwater observation platform to observe underwater conditions in real time.

A Chinese Academy of Science said, “Construction work on the long-term observation platform covering key areas in the South China and East China seas will be done with the help of Shanghai’s Tongji University and the Institutes of Acoustics,”
However, the Institute of Acoustics refused to reveal the exact location and further details of the researches on the platform due to its sensitive nature. After all, If the platform is to be used for natural observation, why would it be classified and sensitive nature?

An underwater platform could be a perfect military outpost because it is not visible by the surface ships and hard to be bombed from the air. This platform could be built with non metallic and sonar absorbing material making it harder to be detected with the actual technologies available.

This remote military platform could house sensors, radar and torpedo to take out any submarines in its proximity.
China has rapidly excelled in construction, in the past 2 years alone, it has virtually convert small reef with rock to a large sand islands of 3000 acres and more. And it has constructed hundreds of off-shore oil platform in the South China Sea.

While United States’ approach in Asia has always been focused on its ability to move aircraft carriers, destroyers and submarines through China’s backyard, Beijing has been doing everything it can to tilt the strategic balance against its possible foes. It’s a strategy that includes new weapons systems, considerable conventional naval expansion but also a host of other tactics including building naval bases, floating power stations, artificial islands and possibly an underwater naval outpost.

How much longer Washington will be able to perform such operations unchallenged, however, only time will tell….But time is running out!


China to build first underwater platform in South China Sea

船都要定期上岸保養除鏽，佢起個建築物嚮水底？祝佢好運啦
